sourceMovie.title: How to Be Single
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: How to Be Single works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Enchanted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Be Single clicked for you, Enchanted (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after How to Be Single?
faq.answer: Try Enchanted, Manhattan, What's Your Number?—each shares DNA with How to Be Single while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is How to Be Single?
faq.answer: How to Be Single (2016) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is How to Be Single worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
